project.name: jobsync
project.description: JobSync is a self-hosted, open-source web app for tracking and organizing a job search, featuring an application tracker, monitoring dashboard, AI resume review and job matching, EURES/ESCO European job discovery automations, and a CRM module for contacts, interviews, tasks, and notes. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: docker-compose.ghcr.yml
nodes.summary: Compose override that switches the app service to pull the published GHCR fork image (pull_policy: always) instead of building from source, layered over docker-compose.yml.
nodes.languageNotes: Compose override file pattern: combined with the base file via -f flags; only redefines pull_policy while inheriting all env/volume/healthcheck wiring from the base.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Primary orchestration file defining the jobsync_app service that builds this repo's Dockerfile, maps port 3737, wires required secrets (AUTH_SECRET, ENCRYPTION_KEY) and module API keys, mounts a /data volume, and runs an HTTP healthcheck.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-service compose with extra_hosts host.docker.internal for Ollama access, env-var defaults via ${VAR:-default}, and a wget-based healthcheck with start_period grace.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build (base → deps → builder → runner) producing a minimal Node 20 Alpine Next.js standalone image that runs as a non-root user on port 3737 via docker-entrypoint.sh.
nodes.languageNotes: Four-stage build separates dependency install, Prisma generate + Next.js build, and a lean runner copying only .next/standalone + static assets; creates a system nextjs:nodejs user and a writable /data volume.

nodes.name: base
nodes.summary: Base stage pinning node:20.18.0-alpine as the shared foundation for all subsequent build stages.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on push and pull requests to main/dev, installing dependencies with bun, generating the Prisma client, then linting, type-checking, running unit tests, and building the Next.js app.
nodes.languageNotes: Single ubuntu-latest job with inline test env vars (DATABASE_URL, AUTH_SECRET) and a bun-dependency cache keyed on bun.lock hash.

nodes.name: ci
nodes.summary: Primary CI job running the full quality gate sequence: install, prisma generate, lint, tsc --noEmit, unit tests, and production build on ubuntu-latest.

nodes.name: docker-publish.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds the fork Dockerfile and publishes a multi-arch (amd64/arm64) image to GitHub Container Registry on pushes to main and version tags, using the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN with packages:write.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses QEMU + Buildx for multi-arch builds, docker/metadata-action for semver tag derivation, gha build cache, and a concurrency group that cancels in-flight builds on newer pushes to the same ref.

nodes.name: build-and-push
nodes.summary: Job that checks out the repo, sets up QEMU and Buildx, logs in to GHCR, extracts image metadata/tags, and builds and pushes the multi-arch Docker image (push gated to non-PR events).

nodes.name: 021-cross-user-module-degradation.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record establishing that module-level degradation (auth failure, circuit-breaker trips) intentionally affects all users' automations for a shared external service, while per-automation degradation and notifications remain per-user.

nodes.name: 022-error-status-inference-heuristics.md
nodes.summary: ADR accepting string-heuristic inference of error/status from external errors as a pragmatic interim solution, documenting the correct long-term typed-error approach and the trade-offs.

nodes.name: 023-usedraggable-over-usesortable-kanban.md
nodes.summary: ADR choosing dnd-kit's useDraggable over useSortable for Kanban cards because the board supports cross-column moves rather than within-column reordering.

nodes.name: 024-compare-and-swap-status-transitions.md
nodes.summary: ADR adopting a compare-and-swap (expected-current-status) guard for Job status transitions to prevent lost updates, instead of full optimistic locking with version columns.

nodes.name: 025-data-enrichment-connector.md
nodes.summary: ADR defining the Data Enrichment Connector architecture: a fallback-chain orchestrator running per-dimension enrichment modules (logo, deep-link) as best-effort, non-blocking operations with cache and audit.

nodes.name: 026-multi-channel-notification-architecture.md
nodes.summary: ADR describing the multi-channel notification architecture via ChannelRouter, the NotificationChannel interface, two-level gating, and webhook delivery specifics (HMAC signing, retry, auto-deactivation).

nodes.name: 027-publishable-key-in-enrichment-urls.md
nodes.summary: ADR justifying embedding publishable (non-secret) API keys directly in enrichment URLs as a low-risk decision, explaining why the credential-defense ADR-016 does not apply and listing hardening measures.

nodes.name: 028-self-contained-modules.md
nodes.summary: ADR establishing the self-contained Module (Manifest v2) pattern: co-located i18n, self-registration, a central import point, and no global module strings.

nodes.name: 029-scope-enrichment-cache-keys-by-userid.md
nodes.summary: ADR mandating that enrichment cache keys include userId to prevent cross-user data leakage between tenants sharing the same enrichment results.

nodes.name: 030-deck-action-contract-and-notification-late-binding.md
nodes.summary: ADR consolidating three decisions: the useDeckStack.onAction contract refinement (createdJobId), the notification late-binding i18n pattern, and the deck action routing invariant requiring all actions to flow through the state machine.

nodes.name: 031-health-escalation-via-degradation-event.md
nodes.summary: ADR deciding to reuse the AutomationDegraded domain event for health escalation rather than introducing a new event type, with mitigations for the semantic overloading.

nodes.name: 032-pii-egress-redaction-shared-leaf.md
nodes.summary: ADR centralizing PII egress redaction in a dependency-free leaf module (src/lib/pii) as the single source of truth for direct-identifier redaction before cloud AI transfer, per GDPR data minimization.

nodes.name: 033-gdpr-data-audit-trail-on-adminauditlog.md
nodes.summary: ADR reusing the AdminAuditLog model via a shared server-only writer to record the GDPR data audit trail, rather than introducing a separate audit table.

nodes.name: 034-user-home-location-and-currency-on-profile.md
nodes.summary: ADR placing the user's home location and preferred currency on the Profile aggregate (with persistence and rationale details) rather than on a separate settings entity.

nodes.name: 035-crm-update-actor-provenance.md

layers.description: React UI — Shadcn components, staging/kanban/crm/automations/settings/profile/admin views, hooks, context, i18n dictionaries, Next.js dashboard page routes/layouts, and client assets (push service worker, flags).

layers.name: Application & Domain Layer
layers.description: Server-action Repositories (src/actions), domain models/value objects (src/models), and supporting library services — events, scheduler, notifications, auth, PII redaction, assets, email/push, salary, CRM status-categories, instrumentation, and public-API helpers — implementing JobSync business logic.

layers.name: Connector / Integration ACL

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Prisma persistence layer — schema.prisma, the SQLite migration history (migrations/*.sql with their table definitions) and seed — defining the relational model for jobs, per-user JobStatus, automations, CRM, salary, profile, notifications, and enrichment.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Containerization and deployment — multi-stage Dockerfile, docker-compose variants, entrypoint/deploy/release shell scripts, and the GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines.

layers.name: Testing Layer

tour.title: What JobSync Is
tour.description: Start with the README to understand JobSync's purpose: a self-hosted job-application tracker built on Next.js 15, Prisma/SQLite and Shadcn UI, with EURES/ESCO European job-discovery automations and a CRM module. This document frames the feature set and gives you the mental map for everything that follows. Read it as the roadmap for the tour ahead.

tour.title: How the App Boots
tour.description: The Next.js App Router root layout is the runtime entry point. It wires the locale provider, theme provider, global styles and client error capture around every page. Alongside it, instrumentation.ts runs once at server startup to pre-warm the holiday reference cache and launch the automation scheduler and CRM cron — establishing the background machinery the rest of the app relies on.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router uses nested layout.tsx files as composition wrappers; instrumentation.ts is a special Next.js hook that runs once when the server process starts, before any request is served — the right place for singletons and warm-up work.

tour.title: Authentication & Session
tour.description: Building on the boot sequence, auth.ts configures NextAuth and exposes the session, while middleware.ts gates routes and strips credential params on a 303 redirect for defense-in-depth. Every server action and API route ultimately derives the trusted user identity from here — the foundation of the IDOR ownership rule (ADR-015) you'll see enforced across the data layer.

tour.title: The Relational Model (Prisma Schema)
tour.description: The Prisma schema is the authoritative data model — ~50 models including User, Job, Automation, StagedVacancy, the CRM core (Person, CrmInterview, CrmTask, CrmNote, JobContact) and notification entities. Understanding these tables and their relations is the single best lens on the whole domain, because every server action below is a typed query over this schema.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma generates a fully-typed client from schema.prisma. src/lib/db.ts exports a singleton PrismaClient (cached on globalThis to survive HMR) so the app never opens redundant connection pools in development.

tour.title: Domain Models & Value Objects
tour.description: JobSync uses DDD idioms. These model files define the ubiquitous language: ActionResult<T> is the typed server-action outcome, job.model.ts holds Job domain logic, and person.model.ts defines the CRM Person aggregate with its value objects (TypedEmail, Address, SocialProfile) and state-machine validators. They are the vocabulary every action and component speaks.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript discriminated unions power ActionResult<T> ({ success: true; data } | { success: false; message }), letting callers narrow the result type after checking the success flag — no exceptions thrown across the action boundary.

tour.title: Server Actions as Repositories (DDD)
tour.description: Server actions are JobSync's Repository layer — one action file per aggregate. job.actions.ts modifies the Job aggregate (job + notes + tags + status) and person.actions.ts owns the CRM Person aggregate. Building on Step 5's models, each returns ActionResult<T> and includes userId in every Prisma where-clause, never trusting client-submitted identity.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js "use server" files expose every export as a browser-callable endpoint. JobSync's security rule: functions accepting a raw userId must NOT live in such files — they belong in 'server-only' modules or must re-derive identity from the session.

tour.title: The Connector / Module ACL
tour.description: All external integrations follow the App ↔ Connector ↔ Module Anti-Corruption-Layer pattern. manifest.ts defines the shared kernel — ModuleManifest variants describing identity, credentials, health and resilience — and registry.ts is the single unified registry storing every module's manifest plus runtime state. register-all.ts wires the central, side-effect-import registration so modules self-register without hardcoded arrays.

tour.title: A Connector in Action: Job Discovery
tour.description: Now see the ACL working end-to-end. The job-discovery runner orchestrates search + matching with credential PUSH, returning the shared domain type DiscoveredVacancy. The EURES module is a concrete implementation: it translates EURES's foreign types (locationCodes, jvProfiles) into the domain language and declares its own manifest, resilience and i18n — a self-contained Bounded Context.

tour.title: Event Bus & Cross-Cutting Subsystems
tour.description: The TypedEventBus decouples subsystems: emitters publish typed events (defined in event-types.ts) and consumers react. The scheduler RunCoordinator is the single mutex-guarded entry point for every automation run, and enrichment-trigger.ts subscribes to CompanyCreated/VacancyPromoted to fire best-effort logo/deep-link enrichment. This is how features stay loosely coupled.

tour.title: The CRM Connection (Welle 3)
tour.description: The CRM is an independent aggregate that relates to Jobs without merging into them. crmInterview.actions.ts and crmTask.actions.ts drive state machines (scheduled→completed, pending→done), jobContact.actions.ts manages the N:M Person↔Job recruiter join, and crm-activity-logger.ts projects domain events into an immutable timeline read model via declarative registerProjection() calls.

tour.title: The Public API v1 & GDPR-Safe AI Egress
tour.description: The /api/v1/* surface is an Open Host Service over the data layer. with-api-auth.ts is the mandatory HOF wrapping every route with CORS, API-key auth, rate limiting and error sanitization, while the jobs route exposes explicit select shapes that never leak internal fields. pii/index.ts is the dependency-free leaf that redacts direct identifiers before any free-text reaches a cloud AI provider — GDPR Art. 5(1)(c).
tour.languageLesson: A Higher-Order Function (withApiAuth) wraps each handler so cross-cutting concerns (auth, rate-limit, CORS, security headers) live in one place instead of being copy-pasted into every route — the functional analogue of middleware.

tour.title: Internationalization Backbone
tour.description: Every UI string is translated across 4 locales (EN/DE/FR/ES). i18n/index.ts is the client adapter (useTranslations, formatters) and i18n/server.ts the server-only counterpart — an adapter pattern that lets consumers stay stable even if the underlying dictionary system changes. The crm.ts dictionary shows a real namespace with ~160 keys. i18n/index.ts has the highest fan-in in the whole graph (167), underscoring how pervasive translation is.

tour.title: The Presentation Layer
tour.description: The dashboard page is the highest-scored code entry point and the visual home, composing widgets like the status funnel and recent-activity cards. The Shadcn-based button.tsx (fan-in 116) is the reusable UI primitive woven through nearly every screen, and StagingContainer.tsx hosts the swipe/deck vacancy-triage experience where discovered jobs become tracked applications.

tour.title: Specs, Containerization & CI
tour.description: JobSync captures domain rules as authoritative Allium specs (module-lifecycle.allium, crm.allium) that are the single source of truth for behavior. The multi-stage Dockerfile packages the app into a lean production image, docker-compose.yml orchestrates the self-hosted deployment, and the CI pipeline ties it together — the project's path from spec to running container.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use multiple FROM stages (deps → builder → runner): the builder compiles with dev dependencies, while the final runner copies only the standalone Next.js output, keeping the production image small and free of build tooling.

tour.title: How It's Tested
tour.description: No code ships without coverage. The Jest suite spans unit, component and integration tests — here exercising the event bus, the CRM Person aggregate's server actions, and the activity-logger projections — while Playwright e2e specs like contact-crud.spec.ts validate critical user paths against real Chromium. This closes the loop: the architecture you toured is pinned by executable contracts.
